revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[arz:ضريح هاكونى]][[de:Hakone-Schrein]][[en:Hakone Shrine]][[fa:معبد هاکونه]][[fr:Hakone-jinja]][[ja:箱根神社]][[th:ศาลเจ้าฮาโกเนะ]][[zh:箱根神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hakone Jinja
箱根神社 | image = File:Hakonejinja -02.jpg | caption = [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] of Hakone Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.20388889|N|139.02555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Hakone Gongen|qid=Q11603375|1=Hakone Gongen}}; {{ill|Hoori|qid=Q1051960|1=Hoori}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|qid=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}; {{ill|Ninigi|qid=Q1056017|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} | founded_by ={{ill|Mangan|qid=Q11353818|1=Mangan}} | established = {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} | date_destroyed = | location = 80-1 Hakone-machi, Ashigarushimo District, Kanagawa | website = {{URL|https://hakonejinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style ={{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Moto-Hakone|qid=Q11388028|1=Moto-Hakone}} }} [[File:HakoneTorii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|[[Torii]] of Hakone Shrine at {{ill|Lake Ashi|en|Lake Ashi|qid=Q187053|lt=Lake Ashi|1=Lake Ashi}}]] [[File:Komainu at Hakone Shrine.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|qid=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}} in Hakone Shrine]] The {{nihongo|'''Hakone Shrine'''|箱根神社|Hakone Jinja}} is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} on the shores of {{ill|Lake Ashi|en|Lake Ashi|qid=Q187053|lt=Lake Ashi|1=Lake Ashi}} in the town of [[:en:Hakone, Kanagawa|Hakone]] in the {{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District|1=Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa}} of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}.Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/hakone-jinja/ "Hakone Jinja"]; retrieved 2013-1-27. It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Hakone [[Gongen]]'''|箱根権現|}}.{{ill|Louis Frédéric|en|Louis Frédéric|qid=Q726040|lt=Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric|1=Louis Frédéric}}. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA279&dq= "Hakone Gongen-jinja"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 279. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Hakone Shrine are * {{nihongo|'''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' |瓊瓊杵尊|}} * {{nihongo| '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' |木花之開耶姫|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Hoori|Hoori-no-Mikoto]]''' |火遠理命|}} They are known collectively as the {{nihongo|Hakone Ōkami|箱根大神|}}. ==History== According to shrine tradition, ''Hakone-jinja'' was founded in 757 during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|qid=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}.The date of Hakone Shrine's foundation is unknown, and although apparently very ancient, its name does not appear in any of the [[Rikkokushi]] official chronicles nor the [[Engishiki]] records. The original shrine was at the summit of the Komagatake peak of {{ill|Mount Hakone|en|Mount Hakone|qid=Q1067417|lt=Mount Hakone|1=Mount Hakone}}.{{cite web|title=御際神、由緒、例祭日|url=http://hakonejinja.or.jp/02-contents/02-main/07-gosaijin-yuisyo/03-contents/01-gosaijin/01-gosaijin-yuisyo.html|publisher=Hakone Shrine|accessdate=7 December 2017|trans-title=Gods worshipped, origins, ceremony days |language=Japanese}} The shrine was relocated to the shores of {{ill|Lake Ashi|en|Lake Ashi|qid=Q187053|lt=Lake Ashi|1=Lake Ashi}}; its current form dates to 1667.Giesen, Walter. (2012). [https://books.google.com/books?id=zmUoDqXbG2QC&pg=PA259&dq= ''Japan,'' p. 259]. Credit for establishment is also given to Priest Mangan, for pacifying the nine-headed dragon that lived at the bottom of {{ill|Lake Ashi|en|Lake Ashi|qid=Q187053|lt=Lake Ashi|1=Lake Ashi}}. According to the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|qid=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=Azuma Kagami}}'', [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] sought guidance and shelter from the kami at Hakone after his defeat in {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|en|Battle of Ishibashiyama|qid=Q2888010|lt=Battle of Ishibashiyama|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama}} during the {{ill|Genpei War|en|Genpei War|qid=Q737542|lt=Genpei War|1=Genpei War}}. Upon becoming shōgun, Yoritomo became a patron of the shrine. In the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was popular with samurai. This support continued through the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}. The shrine was burned down by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the {{ill|Siege of Odawara (1590)|en|Siege of Odawara (1590)|qid=Q2622890|lt=Battle of Odawara|1=Siege of Odawara (1590)}}. It was reconstructed by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} and given a grant of 200 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|qid=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'' of revenue. The [[Tokugawa shogunate]] continued to support the shrine. In the system of Ranked Shinto shrines, Hakone was listed in 1875 among the 3rd class of nationally significant shrines or {{nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei-shōsha]]''|国幣小社}}.{{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan'', p. 125. ==Cultural artifacts== Hakone Shrine has a small museum, which displays a number of the shrine's treasures. These include five items which are Ranked as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. ==Events== The main festival of the shrine is held annually on August 1. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] ==References== {{Reflist}} File:Crowd - Hakone-jinja - Hakone, Japan - DSC05780.jpg File:Hakone-jinja - Hakone, Japan - DSC05819.jpg File:Processional shrines - Hakone-jinja - Hakone, Japan - DSC05744.jpg File:Stairs - Hakone-jinja - Hakone, Japan - DSC05716.jpg File:Ducks & floating torii.jpg File:Avenue - Hakone-jinja - Hakone, Japan - DSC05864.jpg == External links == {{Commons category-inline|Hakone-jinja}} * [https://hakonejinja.or.jp/ 箱根神社 (''Hakone jinja'') website] * [https://web.archive.org/web/20120304094659/http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~qm9t-kndu/hakone.htm "Hakone Jinja Shrine" at Asahi-net.or.jp] * [http://www.lakeashinoko.com (''Lake Ashi'') website] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[en:Hakone Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح هاكونى]] {{Commons category|Hakone-jinja}} [[de:Hakone-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد هاکونه]] [[fr:Hakone-jinja]] [[ja:箱根神社]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าฮาโกเนะ]] [[zh:箱根神社]] {{wikidata link|Q705310}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakone, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
